title: Which countries include mumps vaccines in their national vaccination programs?
subtitle: This shows which countries provide and recommend mumps vaccines through routine services. People may still be able to receive the vaccine if it's not in the routine schedule – it might be optional or available commercially.
caption:
Which countries include mumps vaccines in their national vaccination programs? 2024

This shows which countries provide and recommend mumps vaccines through routine services. People may still be able to receive the vaccine if it's not in the routine schedule – it might be optional or available commercially.

The map displays the global distribution of national vaccination policies regarding mumps immunization as of 2024, categorizing countries by whether the vaccine is administered routinely across the entire nation, not routinely administered, or administered only in specific regions. The data reveals a distinct geographic divide in public health infrastructure. In the Americas, the United States, Canada, Mexico, and the nations of South America are shaded to indicate that the mumps vaccine is part of the routine schedule for the entire country. Similarly, Australia, New Zealand, and the majority of European nations, including the United Kingdom, France, and Germany, fall into this category. In contrast, the African continent is predominantly shaded to show that the vaccine is not routinely administered, covering nations such as Nigeria, Ethiopia, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo. This pattern extends across much of Asia, where India, China, and Indonesia are shown as not routinely administering the vaccine, although Japan and South Korea are exceptions where it is routine nationwide. Greenland appears with a hatched pattern, denoting that no data is available for that territory. The legend specifies that the figures exclude international aviation and notes that improved mumps vaccines first became available in 1967. The visual arrangement shows a concentration of routine administration in the Western Hemisphere and Europe, while large portions of Africa and Asia show a lack of routine national administration.
